description: Extend Diagnostic struct with col, end_line, end_col fields
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/diagnostic.rs
Add three new fields to the Diagnostic struct: col: Option<usize>, end_line: Option<usize>, end_col: Option<usize> (after the existing line field)
Update every Diagnostic struct literal across all rule files to include col: None, end_line: None, end_col: None
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_missing_name.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_missing_desc.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_oversized.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_too_long.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_invalid.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_desc_too_long.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_invalid_shell.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/agent_missing_tools.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_unknown_event.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_legacy_event.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/broken_paths.rs diagnostic construction
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/misplaced_features.rs diagnostic construction
Update any test code that constructs Diagnostic literals to include the new fields
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all existing tests pass
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint issues

description: Extend Rule trait with help_url() and help_text() default methods
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/rule.rs
Add fn help_url(&self) -> Option<&'static str> { None } to the Rule trait (after default_severity, before check)
Add fn help_text(&self) -> Option<&'static str> { None } to the Rule trait (after help_url, before check)
Run cargo build --workspace to verify all existing rule implementations still compile (default methods mean no breakage)
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all existing tests pass
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Add per-field line tracking to Frontmatter struct and parser [DONE]
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/frontmatter.rs
Add field_lines: BTreeMap<String, usize> to the Frontmatter struct (after the fields field)
In the parse() function, change the yaml_block.lines() loop (around line 87) to use .enumerate() to get line indices
Compute absolute 1-based line number for each key as: start_line + 1 + line_index (accounting for the opening --- delimiter)
At every fields.insert(key, value) call site, also insert the key and computed line number into field_lines
Handle multi-line continuation values: only record the line of the initial key, not continuation lines
Update all Frontmatter struct literal constructions in test code to include field_lines: BTreeMap::new() or appropriate test values
Add unit tests: parse frontmatter with 3 fields, verify field_lines maps each key to correct 1-based line
Add unit test: parse frontmatter with leading blank lines, verify field_lines accounts for start_line offset
Add unit test: parse frontmatter with multi-line value, verify field_lines records starting line only
Add unit test: empty frontmatter (--- followed by ---) produces empty field_lines
Run cargo build --workspace
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Update diagnostic sorting to use file_path then line then col
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs
Find the sort_by call at line 131 that sorts only by file_path
Replace with multi-key sort: a.file_path.cmp(&b.file_path).then_with(|| a.line.cmp(&b.line)).then_with(|| a.col.cmp(&b.col))
Add unit test: create diagnostics with same file but different lines, verify sort order
Add unit test: create diagnostics with same file and line but different cols, verify sort order
Add unit test: create diagnostics with None line values, verify they sort before Some values
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Update all 12 rules with help_url(), help_text(), and accurate line numbers from Frontmatter field_lines
steps: Update skill/missing-name (skill_missing_name.rs): implement help_url() returning GitHub docs link, help_text() returning 'add a "name" field to the YAML frontmatter', change line from Some(1) to frontmatter.start_line when field is absent
Update skill/missing-description (skill_missing_desc.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'add a "description" field to the YAML frontmatter', change line to frontmatter.start_line
Update skill/oversized (skill_oversized.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'reduce file size below 15000 characters', keep line as Some(1) since it is whole-file

Update skill/name-too-long (skill_name_too_long.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'shorten the name to 60 characters or fewer', change line to frontmatter.field_lines.get("name").copied()

Update skill/name-invalid-chars (skill_name_invalid.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'use only alphanumeric, hyphen, underscore characters', change line to frontmatter.field_lines.get("name").copied()

Update skill/description-too-long (skill_desc_too_long.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'shorten the description to 200 characters or fewer', change line to frontmatter.field_lines.get("description").copied()
Update skill/invalid-shell (skill_invalid_shell.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'use a supported shell value', change line to frontmatter.field_lines.get("shell").copied()
Update agent/missing-tools (agent_missing_tools.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'add a "tools" field listing required tools', change line to frontmatter.start_line
Update hook/unknown-event (hook_unknown_event.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'use a valid hook event name', keep existing line computation
Update hook/legacy-event-name (hook_legacy_event.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'rename to the PascalCase event name', add line-by-line search of raw JSON to find key line number

Update plugin/broken-paths (broken_paths.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'fix or remove the broken file reference', expose the already-computed pos variable as col: Some(pos + 1) in the Diagnostic
Update source/misplaced-features (misplaced_features.rs): implement help_url(), help_text() returning 'run "aipm migrate" to move into .ai/', keep line as None
Update all corresponding unit tests to verify help_url() returns Some(...) for each rule
Update all corresponding unit tests to verify help_text() returns Some(...) for each rule
Update tests that assert on line numbers to reflect accurate field_lines-based values
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Add annotate-snippets and anstyle dependencies to workspace Cargo.toml
steps: Open the workspace Cargo.toml at the repo root
Add annotate-snippets = "0.11" (or latest stable) to [workspace.dependencies]
Add anstyle = "1" to [workspace.dependencies] (verify version matches clap's transitive dep with cargo tree -p clap -i anstyle)
Add anstyle-query = "1" to [workspace.dependencies] (verify version matches clap's transitive dep)
Open crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml
Add annotate-snippets = { workspace = true } to [dependencies]
Add anstyle = { workspace = true } to [dependencies]
Add anstyle-query = { workspace = true } to [dependencies]
Run cargo build --workspace to verify all dependencies resolve
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Implement Human reporter with annotate-snippets colored output and snippet rendering [DONE]
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/reporter/ directory if converting reporter.rs to a module (or keep inline in reporter.rs)
Define pub struct Human with fields: fs: &dyn Fs (for reading source files), color: ColorChoice enum (Never/Auto/Always), base_dir: PathBuf
Define a ColorChoice enum (or reuse from anstyle-query) with Never, Auto, Always variants
Implement color auto-detection function: check --color flag value first, then NO_COLOR env var, then CLICOLOR=0, then is_atty(stdout)
Implement Reporter trait for Human struct
In the report() method, iterate outcome.diagnostics and render each as a rustc-style block
For each diagnostic: write severity label with color (bold red for error, bold yellow for warning) using anstyle
Write rule_id in brackets after severity (bold)
Write message after colon
Write --> arrow line with file_path:line:col (bold blue for --> and line numbers)
Write | gutter separator (bold blue)
If diagnostic.line is Some and file can be read via fs: read the source file, extract context lines (line-1, line, line+1), render via annotate-snippets Snippet
If diagnostic has col/end_col: render underline carets ^^^ at the column range in the severity color
If diagnostic.line is None (directory-level): skip snippet section, only show --> path
After snippet: write = help: lines for help_text (bold cyan label) if the rule provides help_text()
Write = help: line for help_url (bold cyan label) if the rule provides help_url()
After all diagnostics: write summary line like 'error: N error(s), M warning(s) emitted'
Handle case where fs.read() fails for a file: gracefully skip snippet, still render diagnostic header and help
Add unit test: Human reporter with color=Never produces expected uncolored output for a warning diagnostic
Add unit test: Human reporter with color=Always produces ANSI-colored output with correct escape codes
Add unit test: Human reporter renders snippet when line is Some and file content is available via MockFs
Add unit test: Human reporter skips snippet when line is None (directory-level diagnostic)
Add unit test: Human reporter renders underline carets when col and end_col are present
Add unit test: Human reporter renders help text and help url lines when provided
Add unit test: Human reporter handles missing file gracefully (fs.read returns error)
Add unit test: summary line shows correct counts for mixed errors and warnings
Add unit test: 'no issues found' message when diagnostics is empty
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Extend Json reporter with col, end_line, end_col, help_url, help_text, and severity_code fields [DONE]
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/reporter.rs (or the new reporter module)
In the Json reporter's report() method, find the diagnostic JSON object construction (around lines 67-78)
After the existing 'line' field, add 'col' field: emit as number if Some, null if None
Add 'end_line' field: emit as number if Some, null if None
Add 'end_col' field: emit as number if Some, null if None
Add 'severity_code' field: emit 1 for Error, 2 for Warning (LSP-compatible numeric mapping)

Add 'help_url' field: this requires access to the Rule that produced the diagnostic -- either store help_url/help_text on Diagnostic itself, or look up from a rule registry. Simplest: add help_url and help_text as Optional fields on Diagnostic, populated by rules
Add 'help_text' field: emit as escaped JSON string if Some, null if None
Update escape_json_string if needed for any new characters in help_url/help_text values
Update existing Json reporter unit tests to verify new fields appear in output
Add unit test: Json output includes severity_code 1 for error diagnostic
Add unit test: Json output includes severity_code 2 for warning diagnostic
Add unit test: Json output includes col, end_line, end_col as null when None
Add unit test: Json output includes col as number when Some
Add unit test: Json output includes help_url and help_text when provided
Add unit test: Json output is valid parseable JSON (can parse with serde_json in test)
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Implement ci-github reporter emitting GitHub Actions annotation format [DONE]
steps: Create the CiGitHub struct (zero-sized unit struct) in the reporter module
Implement Reporter trait for CiGitHub
In report(): iterate outcome.diagnostics
For each diagnostic: emit ::{severity} file={file_path},line={line},col={col}::{rule_id}: {message}
Map Severity::Error to 'error', Severity::Warning to 'warning'
Default line to 1 if None, col to 1 if None
No color, no snippets, no summary line -- just annotation commands
Add unit test: CiGitHub reporter produces correct ::error format for error diagnostic
Add unit test: CiGitHub reporter produces correct ::warning format for warning diagnostic
Add unit test: CiGitHub reporter defaults line=1, col=1 when diagnostic has None values
Add unit test: CiGitHub reporter handles special characters in message (no escaping needed for GH format)
Add unit test: CiGitHub reporter with empty diagnostics produces no output
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Implement ci-azure reporter emitting Azure DevOps ##vso[task.logissue] format [DONE]
steps: Create the CiAzure struct (zero-sized unit struct) in the reporter module
Implement Reporter trait for CiAzure
In report(): iterate outcome.diagnostics
For each diagnostic: emit ##vso[task.logissue type={severity};sourcepath={file_path};linenumber={line};columnnumber={col}]{rule_id}: {message}
Map Severity::Error to 'error', Severity::Warning to 'warning'
Default linenumber to 1 if None, columnnumber to 1 if None
No color, no snippets, no summary -- just logging commands
Add unit test: CiAzure reporter produces correct ##vso format for error diagnostic
Add unit test: CiAzure reporter produces correct ##vso format for warning diagnostic
Add unit test: CiAzure reporter defaults linenumber=1, columnnumber=1 when None
Add unit test: CiAzure reporter handles special characters in message (semicolons in particular need care for vso format)
Add unit test: CiAzure reporter with empty diagnostics produces no output
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Update CLI: add --reporter flag, --color flag, deprecate --format as hidden alias [DONE]
steps: Open crates/aipm/src/main.rs
In the Lint variant of the Commands enum, add: reporter: String field with #[arg(long, default_value = "human", value_parser = ["human", "json", "ci-github", "ci-azure"])]
Change existing format field to: format: Option<String> with #[arg(long, hide = true)]
Add: color: String field with #[arg(long, default_value = "auto", value_parser = ["never", "auto", "always"])]
Update the Commands::Lint match arm to destructure the new fields: reporter, format, color
Update cmd_lint function signature to accept reporter: &str, format: Option<&str>, color: &str parameters
In cmd_lint, compute effective_reporter: let effective_reporter = format.unwrap_or(reporter)
Implement color_choice resolution: match color { 'never' => Never, 'always' => Always, _ => auto-detect via anstyle-query or manual TTY/NO_COLOR/CLICOLOR check }
Update the reporter match block to dispatch to Human, Json, CiGitHub, or CiAzure based on effective_reporter
For Human reporter: construct with fs, color_choice, and base_dir
For other reporters: construct zero-sized structs as before
Add integration test: --reporter json produces valid JSON output
Add integration test: --reporter ci-github produces ::error/::warning lines
Add integration test: --reporter ci-azure produces ##vso lines
Add integration test: --format json (deprecated alias) produces same output as --reporter json
Add integration test: --color=never with human reporter produces no ANSI escape codes
Add integration test: unknown --reporter value is rejected by clap value_parser
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Add help_url and help_text fields to Diagnostic struct for reporter access [DONE]
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/diagnostic.rs
Add help_url: Option<String> and help_text: Option<String> fields to the Diagnostic struct
Update all Diagnostic construction sites in rule files to include help_url: None, help_text: None initially
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs run_rules_for_source: after collecting diagnostics from a rule, populate help_url and help_text from the Rule trait methods on each diagnostic
This bridges the gap between Rule trait (which knows help info) and Diagnostic (which reporters consume)
Update all test Diagnostic constructions to include the new fields
Run cargo build --workspace
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Create rule documentation markdown files in docs/rules/
steps: Create docs/rules/ directory structure: docs/rules/skill/, docs/rules/agent/, docs/rules/hook/, docs/rules/plugin/, docs/rules/source/
Create docs/rules/skill/missing-name.md with: rule description, what it catches, how to fix, example of correct frontmatter
Create docs/rules/skill/missing-description.md
Create docs/rules/skill/oversized.md
Create docs/rules/skill/name-too-long.md
Create docs/rules/skill/name-invalid-chars.md
Create docs/rules/skill/description-too-long.md
Create docs/rules/skill/invalid-shell.md
Create docs/rules/agent/missing-tools.md
Create docs/rules/hook/unknown-event.md
Create docs/rules/hook/legacy-event-name.md
Create docs/rules/plugin/broken-paths.md
Create docs/rules/source/misplaced-features.md
Verify each rule's help_url() return value matches the actual file path in the repo

description: Run full test suite, clippy, fmt, and coverage gate to verify all changes
steps: Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint violations
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all tests pass
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)' and verify TOTAL branch coverage >= 89%
If coverage is below 89%, add additional tests targeting uncovered branches
Run all four checks one final time: cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace && coverage report
